description: Note *Not available in 3DEC . The Plastic-Hardening (PH) model is a shear and volumetric hardening constitutive model for the simulation of soil behavior. When subjected to deviatoric loading (e.g., during a conventional drained triaxial test), soils usually exhibit a decrease in stiffness, accompanied by irreversible deformation. In most cases, the plot of deviatoric stress versus axial strain obtained in a drained triaxial test may be approximated by a hyperbola. This feature was discussed by Duncan and Chang (1970) in their well-known “hyperbolic-soil” model, which is formulated as a non-li

property_groups.name: Properties
property_groups.description: Material properties for the 'plastic-hardening' zone constitutive model. Assign the model with 'zone cmodel assign plastic-hardening [range ...]', then set these with 'zone property <keyword> <value> [range cmodel-name plastic-hardening]'.

properties.description: cohesion, c. The default and cut-off (allowable minimum) value is 10^{-5} \cdot p^{ref}.
ultimate dilation angle in degrees, \psi. The default is 0.0.
exponent for elastic moduli, m. Allowable value is 0 \le m \le 0.999.
ultimate friction angle in degrees, \phi. The default and cut-off (allowable minimum) value is 0.001 degrees.
initial minimum principal effective stress, \sigma^{ini}_1. See the note*.
initial median principal effective stress, \sigma^{ini}_2. See the note*.
initial maximum principal effective stress, \sigma^{ini}_3. See the note*.

reference pressure, p^{ref} . A non-zero value should be specified based on the unit of stress/pressure adopted in the model. The most commonly used and highly recommended reference pressure is the standard atmospheric pressure, e.g., 100.0 kPa/1.0e5 if in Pa (or other value compatible to the adopted stress/pressure unit).
secant stiffness, E^{ref}_{50}, at 50% of the ultimate deviatoric stress, q_f, when \sigma_3 = - p^{ref} in a triaxial test. A non-zero value must be specified.
If true, the tension limit is set to 0 in the event of tensile failure. The default is false.

Initial stiffness, E^{ref}_{0}, at zero strain when \sigma_3 = - p^{ref}. It is required that E^{ref}_{0} > E^{ref}_{ur}. If not specified, the default value is E^{ref}_{0} = 3 \cdot E^{ref}_{ur}. This property is needed only when the flag for small-strain stiffness is on, otherwise its value is useless.

tangent stiffness, E^{ref}_{oed}, when \sigma_1 = - p^{ref} in an oedometer test. By default, E^{ref}_{oed} = E^{ref}_{50} if E^{ref}_{oed} is not specified while E^{ref}_{50} has been specified. If E^{ref}_{oed} < 0.5 E^{ref}_{50}, the soil is highly compressible, it is recommended to use the Soft-Soil (SS) model instead.
unloading-reloading stiffness, E^{ref}_{ur}, when \sigma_3 = - p^{ref} in a triaxial test. This value must be greater than 2 \cdot E^{ref}_{50}. By default, E^{ref}_{ur} = 4 \cdot E^{ref}_{50}.
normal consolidation coefficient, K_{nc}. It is not allowed to be less than \nu / (1-\nu), a range 0.5 to 0.7 is common. The default is K_{nc} = 1 - \sin \phi.
dimensionless parameter in the cap yield function, \alpha. Initialized internally. See the note* for an alternative option.
cut-off factor, f_{cut}. The default is 0.1.
factor of the dilation law, F_c, allowable range is 0.0 to 0.25. The default is 0.0.
flag for initialization. If set to on, the internal variables will be recalculated. The default is off.
flag for small-strain stiffness. If set on, small-strain stiffness is activated. The default is off.
over consolidation ratio, OCR. For normally consolidated soil, the value should be input as 1.0. The default is 100.0, which denotes an “apparent” non-cap shear plastic hardening model.
Poisson’s ratio, \nu. The default is 0.2.
hardening modulus for cap hardening, H_c. Initialized internally. See the note* for an alternative option.

Shear strain at which G_s / G_0 = 72.2%, If not specified, the default value is \gamma_{70} = 2 \times 10^{-4}. This property is needed only when the flag for small-strain stiffness is on, otherwise its value is useless.
failure ratio, R_f. The default is 0.9.
tension limit, \sigma^t. The default is 0.0.
initial void ratio, e_{ini}. If the current void ratio is not important, or you do not wish to activate the dilation smoothing technique, just use the default value. The default is 1.0.
allowable maximum void ratio, e_{max} . The default is 999.0, which is not realistic but it assures that dilation smoothing effectively will not be activated.
current loading-unloading bulk modulus, K
current unloading-reloading shear modulus, G
accumulated shear plastic hardening parameter, \gamma^p
accumulated volumetric plastic hardening parameter, \varepsilon^p_v
preconsolidation pressure, p_c. Determined by the current stress, \alpha, and OCR.
current void ratio, e
